rationale: Keep: the citing note explicitly says it “extends narrative-based lending studies that used manual coding,” and the cited 2011 JMR paper is exactly a Prosper narrative study linking identity claims to funding, rates, and two-year repayment outcomes. Given this direct conceptual and empirical overlap (same P2P lending context and borrower text as soft information), the citation is plausibly foundational/comparative rather than incidental.
